05/26/26- Pulled the FID AA rights and sent via docusign to Anthony and Allison. Sent the following MRC: 
Hi Anthony and Allison, I pulled the Fidelity forms needed to grant Allison full access to Anthony’s Fidelity accounts. The documents will first be sent to Anthony for signature, and immediately after he signs, they will be sent to Allison to complete her portion. 
 
To access the documents, you’ll need to enter the access code: SDC1234 and you will also receive a separate verification code sent to the phone number ending in 7448.

04/09/26-I called into BTH and spoke with Nancy to make sure the systematic withdrawal is set up correctly. She said that has been in the que but received, she said she is escalating the request and to check back in 3-5 business days. She tried to say there is no bank information attached, we went back and fourth because there was a bank statement, deposit slip, and voided check all submitted with the systematic withdrawal form. I need to call back next Tuesday and check on this. (ctb)
04/09/26- I called in AIG and spoke with Mona, she said that the new systematic withdrawal request was received but cannot be sent up until the divorce was finalized on their end... I asked for the information that is required to finalize it on their end and she said there was an email sent to BES on 04/01. She is unable to give me the information needed to finalize but put in a request to have the email forwarded to me(2-5 business days) Is this what KSO is working on? She confirmed that the old systematic withdrawal being sent to the joint account has been terminated.

I need to talk to KSO and see what the next steps are, and then give Hal a call to see if we need to send out a one time DIST to fulfill his April DIST.  (ctb)

04/21/26- I was on the phone trying to figure this out with Corebridge Friday, they said that Hal's MAWA is maxed out until March of 27' because of the divorce split of over $200,000 to Jill's account. So, we can't apply for the MAWA, I was also told we will need 12 different forms to have a partial withdrawal for Hal every month. I waited on hold with a supervisor for over 30 minutes and he immediatly hung up on me.

I called back and asked for a supervisor today, I spoke with Raina. She said that Hal will not be eligible for the MAWA because of the divorce split, I checked with her to see if this was coded in the system wrong because it should be a transfer, not a withdrawal. She said no, it was not coded wrong and that is Corebridge always shows a withdrawal for a split. So, in Hal's case, I can either set up a MO systematic withdrawal set up but the funds will be consistent every month, or I can have 12 partial withdrawal forms set up(if he wants different amounts MO). Hal also needs a partial DIST form to cover last months $639.13 that he was missing, in which I cannot submit two different withdrawals request at the same time. Raine said that I should submit the partial withdrawal request and then 1-2 days later I should submit the systematic withdrawal request. I need to Fedex forms to both Hal and Jill by end of the day today. (ctb)

04/23/26- KSO reached out to our internal wholesaler on TUE, 04/21, he gave me a call this morning and asked for a rundown of the situation, the date the divorce decree was submitted, and the date of divorce. He just called back in for me to ask who the original acct owner was and the account type. I told him Hal was the original and IRA. I asked if he could double check that since it is currently coded as a withdrawal, if he could please make sure Hal will not have to pay any income tax on the $221,000 DIST, which was the split. He is going to give me a call back in a little bit after speaking with his back office. (ctb)

04/23/25- I spoke with Tom, our internal wholesaler, twice today. He called in for clarification this morning, he wanted to know the original owner, the type of account, the date of divorce, and the date the divorce decree was submitted. He said he was going to talk to the back office and connect with me later. I called back in this afternoon because I had not heard anything, he called me back and said the reason it was coded as a "withdrawal" instead of a transfer is because Corebridge needs a complete ownership change in order to code it as a transfer. He also said that he believes Hal WILL BE taxed on the full $220,000. He said he believes our only option is to submit a partial withdrawal form every single month but that will reduce his living benefit significantly. This needs to be figured out but I am unsure where to go from here, we may need to get Hal on either BES or ZWB cal to come in and fully go over everything. I asked KSO if we can call back into Tom/ Corebridge tomorrow to get all info set in stone. (ctb)

04/24/26- Hal left me 2 VM's last night, first, he said that they are working on coded it to transfer instead of withdrawal as they have tracked down the transfer to Jill's account so they are working on getting that updated. His next VM said that his MAWA is maxed out for the year and any withdrawal from here on out will be considered an access withdrawal, which again, will lower is lifetime income base. ZWB is calling now to argue. (ctb)

05/07/26- So ZWB brought BES in on this early last week, ZWB and BES have been working relentlessly with Corebridge to get the transfer reversed as Hal would be taxed on all $220,000 something dollars and also would not be able to take any DIST for the remainder of the year. Jim Butsco has been the main internal wholesaler we are working with. There was talks about BES having to tell Hal to take SDC to court because BES said he could not sleep with himself knowing that Hal will be shorted DIST and taxed. ZWB and BES worked something out with Corebridge that they will be able to reverse the transfer (Jill's account will go back to Hal's account). So, they will have to split the MAWA that is being taken out every month, and Hal will probably have to find a way to pay Jill out. **I will most likely have to set up a new systematic withdrawal for the new MAWA to Hal's account, but waiting on direction from BES. (ctb)**

Forms Requiring SupervisionThe list below shows the forms that require supervisory review by a supervisor or designee. To ensure proper supervision, please review the list and select the appropriate supervision path (“Requires Supervision” or “Does not require Supervision”) when submitting additional paperwork in CLIC® for an existing account. Note that if any form in the batch is included on the list, the entire batch requires supervision. Any form that requires a principal or supervisor signature must also be submitted through a supervised trade key.

Forms requiring supervision generally fall into these categories:

Suitability Forms
All versions of Cambridge’s Client Information and Suitability forms, including Trust/Entity, Group, Additional Signers, etc.

For directly held accounts, sponsor maintenance forms requiring supervision include those with financial or control implications—such as beneficiary updates, withdrawal requests to third parties, addition of trading authority, and any forms requiring a Medallion Signature Guarantee
While Cambridge does not require supervision of routine maintenance forms (e.g., address changes or standard first party banking information) we do require that they be maintained in the client file for books and records purposes. tasks.title: RIGHTBRIDGE TEMPLATES
tasks.notes:
**✅** ** Adding Templates toRightBRIDGE**

**BeforeYou Begin:**

There are **16 models** to add

Template IDs: **1489–1504**

* * *

**Steps:**

1. **Log     into CIR2.**
2. On
     the left-hand side, **click “RightBRIDGE.”**
3. Under
     **Investment Wizard Tools**, select **“Template Management.”**
4. Click
     **“Add Shared Template.”**
5. Enter
     the first **Template ID** (starting with 1489).
6. Click
     **“Save Template.”**
    - ⚠️ Important: You
      must hit **Save** before moving to the next template.
7. Repeat
     steps **4–6** for each ID through **1504.**
8. After
     all templates are added, confirm that **all 16 templates** appear in
     the list.

* * *

**OngoingMaintenance:**

- This
     is a **manual process.**
- When
     model changes occur, **Beamer will update** the RightBRIDGE templates.
- You
     will receive a **new shared template number** that must be added
     following the same process above.
- **For     future updates:**
     Delete **all existing templates** first, then re-add all new ones.
- It
     goes quickly, and this ensures **nothing is missed** or left outdated.

* * *

💬 **Need help?**

I’m happy to walk through these steps if needed — and for remote team members,
we can screen share to go through it together!

**Forfuture updates:**
Delete **all existing templates** first, then re-add all new ones.  It
goes quickly, and this ensures nothing is missed or left outdated.

tasks.title: RIGHTBRIDGE
tasks.notes:
**LASTUPDATED: 1/15/24 (zwb)** 

**It isimportant to note that the Rightbridge (RB) platform is onlyavailable to licensed admins/advisors. If you are not licensed you will beunable to complete this process** 

**Rightbridge isalso semi-frequently updated slightly so things may look different then theimages posted below** 

**Zoom in ifunable to read the images due to bluriness** 

1. First, login to https://www.cir2.com/ and
     navigate to the RB tool in the tool bar
2. Once you have entered the RB
     platform you will be presented with 4 options. We are currently not
     concerned with the Annuity or Investment Wizard's so dis regard these two
     options.

1. From this page you can select
     previous cases so you can edit them or copy them, but we are interested in
     the "Create New Case" tab under the product profiler.
2. This will launch the client
     information tab requiring information found on either RT, CLIC, or in the
     RB template created for you. If the client is married the spouses info
     will be required as well.
3. Next, the system will request a
     product to be validated which includes investment objectives, time
     horizon, product (advisory/commission account), amount, and fee. Check the
     RB template as well as CLIC for this information.
4. We then need to select the
     account(s) that we are transferring from, which will create additional
     pages for each account. The 3 I have selected are the most common we use
     when transferring to a traditional IRA. We also use "Cash/Current
     Income" for new ROTH IRAs frequently, but this does not require
     additional info besides the amount.
    1. 401k:
        1. If you
       know the name of the 401k plan, you can search for it to input known
       information, otherwise fill the account fees to the best of your
       ability. Typically, we don't have access to participant fees.
        2. As for
       the account features, below is a general template to follow as this
       applies to most people that want to transfer their money to us. However,
       adjust as needed.
    2. Advisory
      IRA:
        1. For
       advisory accounts, look at the statements for the account that is
       transferring for fee information. While the fee % most likely won't be
       listed, we can find the amount pulled and do the math to determine the
       fee %. (If you are unsure you can discuss with the advisor)
        2. For the
       fund fee, you can guesstimate the average if there are a large amount of
       mutual funds, or lookup the funds to calculate the amount. If it is all
       stocks then the fund fee is 0%.
        3. For
       account feature, adjust as needed.
    3. Brokerage
      IRA: 
        1. Brokerage
       IRAs have the same account features as Advisory, but the fee questions
       are different. Update as needed based off the statements or info from
       advisor.

1. The financial details page only
     has NIA currently which should be listed in the template or CLIC
2. Client preferences is a case by
     case basis, although you can find some of this info in CLIC and the
     following images are the typical responses for our clients. Discuss with
     the advisor if you have questions.
    1. Risk tolerance should be available
      in CLIC, liquidity is the focus unless we are doing an annuity, and for
      advice we put someone else managing it a majority of the time.
    2. Adjust fee sensitivity if you find
      it relevant, and insurance need should be "The client does not
      desire a product with death benefits" unless we are doing an annuity
      or insurance product. This RB is only focused on the one account, so it
      will give you an error if you want death benefits with an advisory
      account because you are unable to meet this need.
    3. For form CRS delivery date put the
      day the ppwk was signed or is going to be signed.
3. On the "Results" page,
     you may be required to provide additional notes based on which product(s)
     you are moving from, the new account you are opening, and how the
     questions were answered.
    1. Below is a typical error that
      shows up along with the usual response when a new client is switching
      advisors.

“Please provide additional rationale for
the selected recommendation” – Not happy with current advisor/investment
options

    1. Below
      is an example of a more in depth error that may occur, the test client is
      55 and thus the info is relevant to their situation. Discuss with advisor
      if you need clarification for the notes.

“Please
provide additional rationale for the selected recommendation” – Client has
additional outside accounts to pull from, and is not concerned with potential penalties
and would rather have broader investment options.

1. Once 
     you have completed all required notes, Click on the case summary button on
     the left of the screen.
2. Click the dropdown arrow for
     "Product Profiler" and select "Preview Product Profiler
     PDF" to download the RB. Provide to whoever needs the RB and copy to
     the template used to request to a RT note.

**A fewkey reminders:**

- For **advisory     accounts**, since we use **Nick Raiche and Horizons** in our model
     management selection, we should be selecting **Option 3 or 4** for
     objectives & preferences.

Prefers a broad mix of investment with oversight form an external
fund manager or team of managers that can actively make portfolio changes

Prefers a higher level of oversight beyond fund managers, including
fund screening and control over fund reallocation.

- For **commission-based,     directly held accounts** (mostly buy & hold), RightBRIDGE should
     reflect that. Please be sure to choose **one of the two circled options**
     for those.

The client would like to make their own decisions

The client will make their own decision, but would like
suggestions

-

items.title: Geiger, Jill - ACH & DIST FORMS BTH/AIG Retirement
items.notes:
Name of Client: Jill Geiger
Type of Account: Retirement
Sponsor Company: BTH/AIG
Type of Paperwork Being Pulled: Existing Account Maintenance

Type of Maintenance: 

Notes: 04/08/2026 - Per JEK, she wants to take half of what Hal's getting each month from AIG & BTH. Need to link bank as well. (kso)

04/10/26- I pulled BTH and AIG systematic withdrawal forms, I need to add tax w/h & banking information. Also pulled W-4R for AIG tax withholding. Katie also has an annuity disclosure form up there for sigs as well. (ctb)

I called Jill and she is coming in MON to sign. (ctb)

04/14/26- Jill came in and signed with KSO yesterday, KKB said she was very confused. I confirmed with BES that we are going to use 16% Fed and 4% state for the time being but we could be changing within the next year or two. I reached out to KSO to confirm amounts of DIST. 

I gave Jill a call, apologized that I missed her, let her know we are sticking with the 16% Fed 4% state for the time being but will reevaluate within the next couple years. Told her the DISTs will be sent the second week of every month. (ctb)

04/14/26- I submitted to both AIG and BTH today with the voided check and W-4R attached as well as WC. I sent a secure email for AIG and BTH I used JEK faxage. (ctb)

05/04/26- I asked BES if I should cancel this systematic withdrawal request, he said yes. I called into BTH and spoke with Sherry, she was able to stop the request. I am unsure how to communicate this to Jill. I will get with BES for the communication piece. (ctb)

BES to going to call Jill to explain - I will follow up with BES and make sure the call is made(ctb)

05/07/26- So ZWB brought BES in on this early last week, ZWB and BES have been working relentlessly with Corebridge to get the transfer reversed as Hal would be taxed on all $220,000 something dollars and also would not be able to take any DIST for the remainder of the year. Jim Butsco has been the main internal wholesaler we are working with. There was talks about BES having to tell Hal to take SDC to court because BES said he could not sleep with himself knowing that Hal will be shorted DIST and taxed. ZWB and BES worked something out with Corebridge that they will be able to reverse the transfer (Jill's account will go back to Hal's account). So, they will have to split the MAWA that is being taken out every month, and Hal will probably have to find a way to pay Jill out. **I will most likely have to set up a new systematic withdrawal for the new MAWA to Hal's account, but waiting on direction from BES. (ctb)**

items.title: Bochi, Donna 05/27 1pm (G) || GBU Contract Maturity
items.notes: her policy is up, and GBU rates just went up to 5.2. donna came in and I met with her, she was lovely! we are doing another 5 year contract at the 5.2 rate. she asked if she could add anymore money, and I let he rknow she has 90 days too do so. she is considering it since she has a lot of extra cash, but she is planning on using her cash (400k) to purchase a house whenevr they come across one. she said she has been looking for a couple of years, I asked her if she thinks she will find one within the next year or two and she said yes. so, I let her know aboout tying it up in GBU, but that she will also have another contract up next year in 2027 that is IRA $. she came in today to sign the new app, and I made a copy for her to take home. chey is working on this! she also told me about this fruit/veggie market called Ciolino Fruit & Vegetable Market and we chatted about that for a while. she is a big gardener!!!

items.title: Gardull Jr., Anthony "Tony" J - PPWK FID ACCOUNT ACCESS & CIDA
items.notes:
Name of Client: Anthony "Tony" J Gardull Jr 
Type of Account: Other
Sponsor Company: FID
Type of Paperwork Being Pulled: Existing Account Maintenance

Type of Maintenance: ["AA Rights"]

Notes: 05/26/26- Anthony is probably going to get deployed soon he beleives and wants to get his wife added to all acounts. He wants to give her access to take out DIST without his approval. I confirmed back and he will keep that account on file. He wants to give her access to everything, call on his behalf and and be able to view FID accounts. He mentioned they are getting the POA on file soon but it is not complete yet. (ctb)

05/26/26- I spoke with Sonny on our FID service team. He said that just the account access rights with full trading access will work and give her full access of account, she just cant make changes to things such as beneficiaries. (ctb)

05/26/26- Pulled the FID AA rights and sent via docusign to Anthony and Allison. Sent the following MRC: 
Hi Anthony and Allison, I pulled the Fidelity forms needed to grant Allison full access to Anthony’s Fidelity accounts. The documents will first be sent to Anthony for signature, and immediately after he signs, they will be sent to Allison to complete her portion. 
 
To access the documents, you’ll need to enter the access code: SDC1234 and you will also receive a separate verification code sent to the phone number ending in 7448. 
 
Allison, there will also be a section for you to fill in your SSN. 
 
Please let me know if you have any questions or run into any issues completing the forms and have a great night! - Collin

05/27/26- Tony MRC'd in last night and said... "Hi Collin. Thank you. I need to correct her birthdate 5/14/86 and update email address alliegardull@gmail"

I repulled the AA rights with the updated information and updated RT. I resent the docusign to them and send the following MRC: Good morning Anthony and Allison — I’ve updated Allison’s birthdate and email address and have resent the Fidelity paperwork to the updated email. Everything else in the process will remain the same, including the access code and phone verification. 
 
Please let me know if you have any trouble accessing or completing the forms and I’d be happy to help. Have a great day! - Collin

05/29/26- They signed early this morning, it was auto submitted to FID and I submitted to WC. I will follow up next week that this was processed correctly. (ctb)

05/29/26- I originally pulled the Suit, PER JTOD app, and the cambridge ACH update PER nonretirement per Brooke from Redwood. I got with KSO and she texted PP to see if ACH ppwk was correct. KSO said to pull the PER ACH ETF Instructions instead. I called and left Jon a VM, he called back for me. I am docusigning this over to him although he has a lot to fill out. He asked the pros and cons of contributing with a check, I said if he is just going to contribute once then we will use a check. I said when he wants to take money out it will take longer because it will be sent out via check. And to link the bank pros is easier money movement and more convienent if he plans to take DIST/ CONT often. he was leary about linking his bank it seemed but he wants to do it. I put together the docusign and he will have to fill out bene information, bank information, attachment of voided check, and signatures. I got with ZWB to see how Jimbo does docusign. he said he normally helps him figure it out and I am good to send to Jim with no access code. i sent the following MRC to John after sending the docusign (we dont have Kathryn's phone number.) Hi John, this is Collin with Jim Bunke’s office. I just sent the documents to both of your email addresses. The documents will first be sent to you, and immediately after you sign, they will be sent to Kathryn. When you log in, a verification code will be sent via text message to your cell phone for both signatures. The information needed includes signatures, basic beneficiary information, bank information, and attaching a voided check. I understand this is a lot to fill out, so if it’s easier, I can also mail the documents to your address instead. Please don’t hesitate to reach out if you have any questions! (sent by Collin Berryman) delivered 12:12 pm. (ctb)

Need to get sigs and info from Jon, pull RB, and add to WIP.

items.bucketName: In WIP / Re-Assign RT to ACS/EJD
items.notes:
04/22/26 - Kimberly met with Brent this week. I don't have a review cover, but some notes on a scratch piece of paper. This FID BL did not auto open, but the account number is listed above. Same fee schedule as Kevin (BP). He has notes re: Promedica Roth IRA, $50/MO, was maxing out. I will get with him for more details, but wanted to get this added.

Her FID info - KSheehy2224 | Cole12989!

Kevin's VNG info - KSheehy42 | TobyDog2242!

MRB / JK are calling them to come in next week to log in and make some changes to his BP stuff and move $$ into her BL, FYI! get with them on when they are scheduled! (acs)

04/27/2026 - I pulled the following:
- Cambridge Investment Research Advisors Form CRS
- Cambridge Investment Research, Inc. Form CRS
- Form ADV Delivery Receipt
- Client Information and Suitability Form
- Agreement for Investment Management Services
- RIA BrokerageLink Authorization & Termination
-FIWS RIA Authorization Form

Printed, flagged and gave to JEK to call. (kso)

05/04/2026 - Per JEK - send via DocuSign.  She also has a 403B ROTH #653-541310.  I added that account to CLIC, pulled paperwork and sent everything via DocuSign.  Sent Kimberly following message:
Good Afternoon, Kimberly!

Per your conversation with Jaiden last week, we will be sending two DocuSign envelopes to you.

The first envelope is to add Brent and SD Capital as advisor on your ProMedica 403(b) account.  The second envelope is to add Brent and SD Capital as advisor on your ProMedica 403(b) Roth account.

When opening each envelope, you will be prompted to enter an access code. The code is: **SDC1234**. After entering that code, you will receive a second verification code sent to your cell phone.

Please let us know if you have any questions or if you run into any issues accessing the documents. (kso)

05/14/2026 - I had to resend - they are NOT 403B - they are 401K.  FML.  Resent via DocuSign. (kso)

items.notes:
Name of Client: KEith Tuttle
Type of Account: Other
Sponsor Company: ALL accounts haha except 529s- I have a doc of his accounts to make this easier!
Type of Paperwork Being Pulled: Existing Account Maintenance

Type of Maintenance: ["Beneficiary Change"]

Notes: SO, Ben Sutter sent me very specific instructions in a word doc for all bene designations. I have a breakdown of his current accounts and bene designations, but looks like we need to update everything wiht this doc. The SEP has special instructions, the IRA's and annuities have sepcific instructions with a place to fill in the account number, and the joint accts do as well. I will attached this doc

05/05/26- I called in and spoke with Liza at JNL,  I am to write "Custom beneficiaries attached" and attach the custom intructions. Fidelity allows custom instructions as well with the box on the bene form. 

05/06/26- I have pulled the FS bene update form, FS account maintenance form, Jackson bene designation supplement, FID HSA for 656597975 and 656597974 separately because they have different custom instructions. For DL I need to pull the per stirpes form, trust is primary, the 3 boys are set up as per stirpes and I will set up Kristins portion (25%) as her trust which is listed in the document. DL is the only company that Keith has that does not allow custom bene's.  I am going to get Keith's signature tomorrow on all sponsor companies, as well as a sig on the bottom of all of the custom instructions that Ben sent over.

Katie and I spoke with Ben Sutter: I asked him the following questions...

Can you send us the trust? Yes, of course but it is not fully set up yet. He will send over when complete

Does the trust set up as primary suffice? Or are contingents needed if the trust is primary. Contingents are needed either way.

Can you walk me through how Delaware Life needs set up? Trust primary, three boys are contingents at 25% a piece per stirpes, make Kristen's trust 25% for her share since she is not per stirpes, Ben said she will be able to take the funds out of trust if needed.

Was the trust ammended? If so, can you please send over so we can get updated at FID trust account and also Street Benefit Trust account? Ben will send the ammended trust over when it is finished.

Can I change Ben's document he sent over? I need to add DOB & SSN for bene's. YES we can edit that document however is needed including wrong account numbers.

!!! Ben also said that we are updating the KEITH A. TUTTLE MARITAL TRUST.... not the regular trust. Ben called this his "sub trust".

Just as another note, Keith also has a FS account maintence form that he needs to sign in order to receive checks for his dividens. I called into FS yesterday to make sure he was only receiving dividends and not capital gains, FS confirmed he was only receiving checks for dividends. (ctb)

05/07/26- Keith ended up coming into the office to meet with BES, I had all the ppwk ready to go and got signatures. Kathy did not come in, so I am sending Keith with a folder with the ppwk she needs to sign as well as a return envelope.

Tracking # for USPS-  9405550105797024752876

Gave Keith return envelope to get Kathy sigs and send back to us. (ctb)

05/11/26- I received the bene update forms back from Keith, I sent him a text letting him I know I received and would submit everything as soon as the trust is sent over from Sutter. Keith was appreciative. (ctb)

items.title: Geiger, Hal - ACH UPDATE Fidelity | BTH | AGL
items.notes:
Name of Client: Geiger, Hal
Type of Account: Retirement
Sponsor Company: Fidelity | BTH | AGL
Type of Paperwork Being Pulled: Existing Account Maintenance

Type of Maintenance: ["ACH Update"]

Notes: HE DID NOT PROVIDE DOCUMENTATION OF BANK ACCOUNT:
Bay Area Credit Union
Routing: 241280883
Account: 10004165920093
(there is a joint account account ending in **0095 that needs to be removed and replaced with the account above)

Systematic Withdrawal @ BTH - $1779.19/monthly - you need to call to see if the correct bank is on file, and if not, get the correct form to update banking to HIS account **0093

Systematic Withdrawal @AIG - $3195.63/monthly - you need to call to see if the correct bank is on file, and if not, get the correct form to update banking to HIS account **0093

No periodic plans @ FID - bank needs updated.

03/26/26- Submitted SPI to FID and WC

- Your document(s) have been successfully submitted.
- Confirmation #: EDS202603260054213121

Called Hal to see if he had a preference on the date of DIST, he said same as before. I called into BTH and spoke with Ahmed, he said the current date is the 4th with a 16/4 tax w/h. Called into AIG and spoke with Jon, he said it is processed on the 10th and 16/4 tax w/h. I need a W-4R form to withhold 16% at AIG, so I texted Hal to see if he wanted to stop by the office or me to send out to his address. I faxed BTH ppwk using JEK faxage. (ctb)

TO DO:

- Get AIG W-4R form signed/ Done
- How to replace AIG systematic withdrawal? / It will replace automatically, I called in and spoke with Reagan
- Submit to AIG w/ systematic withdrawal, bank statement, and W-4R/ Submitted to AIG via secure email with W-4R & bank statement on 03/31/26 (ctb)
- FOLLOW UP

04/09/26-I called into BTH and spoke with Nancy to make sure the systematic withdrawal is set up correctly. She said that has been in the que but received, she said she is escalating the request and to check back in 3-5 business days. She tried to say there is no bank information attached, we went back and fourth because there was a bank statement, deposit slip, and voided check all submitted with the systematic withdrawal form. I need to call back next Tuesday and check on this. (ctb)
04/09/26- I called in AIG and spoke with Mona, she said that the new systematic withdrawal request was received but cannot be sent up until the divorce was finalized on their end... I asked for the information that is required to finalize it on their end and she said there was an email sent to BES on 04/01. She is unable to give me the information needed to finalize but put in a request to have the email forwarded to me(2-5 business days) Is this what KSO is working on? She confirmed that the old systematic withdrawal being sent to the joint account has been terminated.

I need to talk to KSO and see what the next steps are, and then give Hal a call to see if we need to send out a one time DIST to fulfill his April DIST.  (ctb)

04/21/26- I was on the phone trying to figure this out with Corebridge Friday, they said that Hal's MAWA is maxed out until March of 27' because of the divorce split of over $200,000 to Jill's account. So, we can't apply for the MAWA, I was also told we will need 12 different forms to have a partial withdrawal for Hal every month. I waited on hold with a supervisor for over 30 minutes and he immediatly hung up on me.

I called back and asked for a supervisor today, I spoke with Raina. She said that Hal will not be eligible for the MAWA because of the divorce split, I checked with her to see if this was coded in the system wrong because it should be a transfer, not a withdrawal. She said no, it was not coded wrong and that is Corebridge always shows a withdrawal for a split. So, in Hal's case, I can either set up a MO systematic withdrawal set up but the funds will be consistent every month, or I can have 12 partial withdrawal forms set up(if he wants different amounts MO). Hal also needs a partial DIST form to cover last months $639.13 that he was missing, in which I cannot submit two different withdrawals request at the same time. Raine said that I should submit the partial withdrawal request and then 1-2 days later I should submit the systematic withdrawal request. I need to Fedex forms to both Hal and Jill by end of the day today. (ctb)

04/23/26- KSO reached out to our internal wholesaler on TUE, 04/21, he gave me a call this morning and asked for a rundown of the situation, the date the divorce decree was submitted, and the date of divorce. He just called back in for me to ask who the original acct owner was and the account type. I told him Hal was the original and IRA. I asked if he could double check that since it is currently coded as a withdrawal, if he could please make sure Hal will not have to pay any income tax on the $221,000 DIST, which was the split. He is going to give me a call back in a little bit after speaking with his back office. (ctb)

04/23/25- I spoke with Tom, our internal wholesaler, twice today. He called in for clarification this morning, he wanted to know the original owner, the type of account, the date of divorce, and the date the divorce decree was submitted. He said he was going to talk to the back office and connect with me later. I called back in this afternoon because I had not heard anything, he called me back and said the reason it was coded as a "withdrawal" instead of a transfer is because Corebridge needs a complete ownership change in order to code it as a transfer. He also said that he believes Hal WILL BE taxed on the full $220,000. He said he believes our only option is to submit a partial withdrawal form every single month but that will reduce his living benefit significantly. This needs to be figured out but I am unsure where to go from here, we may need to get Hal on either BES or ZWB cal to come in and fully go over everything. I asked KSO if we can call back into Tom/ Corebridge tomorrow to get all info set in stone. (ctb)

04/24/26- Hal left me 2 VM's last night, first, he said that they are working on coded it to transfer instead of withdrawal as they have tracked down the transfer to Jill's account so they are working on getting that updated. His next VM said that his MAWA is maxed out for the year and any withdrawal from here on out will be considered an access withdrawal, which again, will lower is lifetime income base. ZWB is calling now to argue. (ctb)

05/07/26- So ZWB brought BES in on this early last week, ZWB and BES have been working relentlessly with Corebridge to get the transfer reversed as Hal would be taxed on all $220,000 something dollars and also would not be able to take any DIST for the remainder of the year. Jim Butsco has been the main internal wholesaler we are working with. There was talks about BES having to tell Hal to take SDC to court because BES said he could not sleep with himself knowing that Hal will be shorted DIST and taxed. ZWB and BES worked something out with Corebridge that they will be able to reverse the transfer (Jill's account will go back to Hal's account). So, they will have to split the MAWA that is being taken out every month, and Hal will probably have to find a way to pay Jill out. **I will most likely have to set up a new systematic withdrawal for the new MAWA to Hal's account, but waiting on direction from BES. (ctb)**

-Log into BES CollegeDirect 529 and establish accounts for Izzy & Bobbi - see if Eli has one that we can re-open, if not, establish a new account for him.
-Once accounts are open - we need to initiate transfer.  Per ChatGTP:
Best method: Initiate from Ohio Direct (pull the funds)
Log in to your Ohio Direct 529

Choose “Roll over from another 529 plan”

Provide:

BlackRock plan name

Account number

Current custodian info

Upload or complete Ohio’s Incoming Rollover Form

Ohio will request the funds directly from BlackRock

Eli BRD #296285
Emily BRD #5056239
Isabel #594838

1️⃣ Open the Ohio Direct 529 (CollegeAdvantage Direct Plan)
Ohio’s Direct 529 plan is called CollegeAdvantage.

How to open it

Go to the Ohio 529 CollegeAdvantage Direct Plan website

Click “Open an Account”

Choose:

Account owner (you)

Beneficiary (child/grandchild/etc.)

Complete:

SSNs

Address/contact info

Bank info (optional now)

Select investments (you can keep this simple—age-based or target enrollment is common)

Submit and wait for the account number (usually immediate or within 1–2 days)
1.

items.title: Beard, Brian - PENNMUTUAL SURRENDER
items.notes:
08/13/2025 - FJD review cover noted Cash Out Rachel PM WL and Leah's PM WL - send forms to Brian Beard. The statement is attached to this activity. I'm not familiar with Penn Mutual and not clear if both Brian and Andrea have separate policies? The statement that was given to me after the review meeting was Penn Mutual Policy 0082705460

Rachel's parents to cash out Penn Mutual whole life policies and distribute funds to their daughters (Leah and Rachel (ejd)

08/14/2025 - There are two separate policies - one for Leah & one for Rachel. I called and spoke with Sarah to verifiy surrender forms for their specific policies and PennMutual is telling me that **Frank needs his licensing updated and is currently not active**.  I was able to get information regarding both Leah and Rachel's policies.  I will send over the surrender forms to Brian, as he is the owner of them - we turned off the monthly auto premium payment - should they change their minds and keep the policies, APL will kick in after 60 days to keep policy from lapsing.  ALSO - they say it is a 15-18 day processing turnaround time. She also said that they will calculate cost basis and they MAY receive tax doc for reportable transaction.  And since they removed from auto pay, they will receive a quarterly bill via the mail, which they can disregard.

Completed forms can be emailed to lifeservicesrequests@pennmutual.com.

I cannot find the form on the site - reached out and requested the form that Sarah said I needed:

**Fixed Products**: Use Form PM6529FIXED (rev 01/25). (kso)

08/18/2025 - Sent paper forms to Brian via USPS regular mail. (kso)
